metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_3010
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.2: "Calponin and caldesmon immunoreactivity are postsynaptic."
- "(a-c) Calponin-immunolabelling is particularly enriched in dendritic spines (Sp) where it is localised between the sacks of the spine apparatus (large arrow), and in the plasma membrane of neurones such as the cell cortex (small arrows) and the post-synaptic density (white arrow). Acidic Cp-immunoreactivity is present as scattered deposits in the subsynaptic web of both asymmetric (b) and symmetric (c) synapses suggesting that the function of this protein is not dependent on the type of synapses.". Experimental description: - "For electron microscopy adult male Wistar rats (220-250 g) were perfused..."
- Although the paper states Calponin, there are 3 calponin gene paralogs. The antibody used in this paper is traced back to PMID:10457177 where the antibody was raised to peptide 'EYPDEYPREYQYGDDQ' that is derived of Calponin-3. In addition Calponin-3 expression seems restricted to the CNS whereas Calponin-1 and 2 are expressed in smooth muscle and non-muscle cells, respectively (see PMID:17768079).
